mcqs.question: Which of the following best describes horizontal scaling?

options.text: Adding more resources to a single server
Adding more servers to a system

mcqs.explanation: Horizontal scaling refers to adding more nodes or servers to a system, unlike vertical scaling which involves adding resources to a single server.

mcqs.question: Which technology enables multiple virtual machines to run on a single physical server?
mcqs.explanation: Virtualization technology allows multiple virtual machines with different operating systems to run on a single physical server, managed by a hypervisor.

mcqs.question: Which characteristic of cloud computing allows users to automatically provision computing capabilities as needed?
mcqs.explanation: On-demand self-service allows users to automatically provision computing capabilities without requiring human interaction with the service provider.

mcqs.question: What is the primary advantage of using a Content Delivery Network (CDN) in scalable internet computing?
mcqs.explanation: CDNs reduce latency by distributing content geographically closer to end users, improving performance and user experience.

mcqs.question: Which scalable computing model is characterized by geographically distributed resources and resource sharing across organizational boundaries?
mcqs.explanation: Grid computing involves geographically distributed resources from different organizations working together to form a virtual supercomputer.
